about.label: About Me
about.bio.p1: I'm a software engineer based in Valencia who has spent over a decade building products on the web — and still finds it genuinely exciting. I started as a junior developer and grew into technical leadership, but I never left the keyboard behind. Code is where I do my best thinking.
about.bio.p2: I care deeply about the quality of what I ship. Not just that it works, but that it's built to last — clean architecture, comprehensive testing, and a codebase the next engineer will actually want to open. I've seen what happens when those things are skipped, and I've learned to treat them as non-negotiable.
about.bio.p3: Outside of work, I'm drawn to languages — I speak Spanish, English, and some French — and I'm always curious about how systems think, whether that's software or something else entirely.

projects.label: Projects
projects.jp-astro-components.description: A reusable component library for Astro projects focused on typography, layout, and content components.
projects.jp-astro-components.detail: jp-astro-components is an open-source component library built for Astro projects. It provides a set of ready-to-use, fully typed components covering typography, layout primitives, and content display — designed to integrate seamlessly with any Astro site without imposing a design system. The library follows a zero-dependency philosophy: components are plain Astro files that you own once installed, with no runtime overhead.
projects.radioassist.description: Dictation application for automatic report generation tailored to breast radiologists, powered by AI.
projects.radioassist.detail: RadioAssist is a full-stack dictation platform designed specifically for breast radiologists. It allows radiologists to dictate findings verbally and automatically generates structured diagnostic reports, reducing manual effort and improving consistency across reporting. The application features a React frontend with real-time transcription feedback, backed by a Node.js/Express API that processes audio input, applies clinical report templates, and persists structured data via Prisma and PostgreSQL. The project was built with a focus on clinical accuracy, fast turnaround, and a clean interface optimised for daily clinical workflows.
